title: ageratina brandegeeana
intro: Ageratina brandegeeana, also known as White Sage or Tree Sage, is a perennial shrub native to Mexico and parts of the southwestern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to act as a natural antiseptic. The health benefits of Ageratina brandegeeana include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis, due to its expectorant properties. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and support overall well-being.
